transcript.text: Brian, good, how are you?
I know we haven't met yet.
I'm Andy.

So, yeah, I think Marcel should be joining.
But I'm just here as part of the conversation as well.
So nice to meet you.
Oh, yeah, nice to meet you.
And thanks for grabbing some time before the holidays.
know things are quieter, but also maybe a little bit more hectic this week.
Just always kind of crazy launching something new.
Yeah, yeah.
How's it going?
I know you guys got the website live.
Last week, two weeks ago.
So how are things looking?
Yeah, it was about two weeks ago.
Overall, things are looking pretty good.
The people can buy the product, so that feels really positive.
We're cooking products, so that feels really good.
That's kind of like first and foremost.
We're dealing with some challenges as it relates to just the media buying side, where all the metrics are looking generally good.
It's like people are engaging with the content that we've created.
People are post-click buying the products that we created, so that all feels good.
What's tough is the media environment is like insane right now, and we're trying to understand, is it something we're doing, or is it like behind the scenes at Meta, they're pulling certain strings?
Because as you're probably aware, I'm also a consultant, so I have visibility.
I have access to all these ads accounts, and I have a good sense of across different verticals.
Like where media trades at, also like relative to the audience we're going after.
And we're just, our CPMs are like at a number that is unfathomable.
Like it just doesn't actually like make any sense, like neuro sense.

And so we were able to get a hold of someone at Netta that we're talking to in like two hours, just to be like, like either tell us what we're doing wrong, or there's something on your end that is like,
It's but it's one or the other, and it seems like it's something that should be fixable, but we won't have a business if we have to pay this amount on Meta, basically.
So that's the thing that's keeping me up at night.
That's very, very frustrating.
Because if I had normalized CPM rates, we would give Meta an infinite amount of money.
That's basically how things are looking right now.
So right now, just so I understand, like, are you guys almost 100% Meta?
Are you guys doing any, like, collect stuff, like, pay search or anything else?
Yeah, a little bit.
Like, we just started with that stuff, and the challenge with that is always, it's like, out of the gates, it's a little bit of a game of whack-a-mole.
Because basically, like, Google throws the firehaves out there, and they give you a lot of garbage, and so you have to, like, cull through that.
So that takes, like, a little bit of time.
I'm not, like, too concerned about that.
I think that'll be like a fine channel for us, but it's going to take like a couple of months to kind of get there.
Because of what we're seeing on Meta, it's like, it's just sort of forcing our hand into other, call it more like upper funnel conversion oriented media.
The reason why this is like keeping us up at night is for better or for worse, like Meta has the best platform.
Like there's just no other platform for this type of media buying that's as good.
It doesn't mean that we can't get the others to perform.
It's just, it is the best.
And so it's like where you start, it's where you get your learnings.
Then you sort of extend that across other platforms.
And it's like, we just haven't had the luxury of figuring, like learning a lot because we're just not seeing the returns that we need and haven't been able to scale it.
And I know like a lot of my experience is more on the B2B side, but this is where I think like content can connect it to this stuff.
Like the way that there might be.
Some opportunities on doing like, for instance, like bid modifiers.

Let's say like card abandoners and things like that.
And then you're capturing that audience as a retargeting bid modifier when they're searching for, you know, even if it's not the funnel, but then that audience you care about to pulling them back in.
And then it's actually worth it because you've already spent some money on them and, you know, and things like that.

And we're spending about half a million a month, which is, you know, for anything direct to consumer is not crazy, but for, you know, was still like a decent sizable thing.

Outside of that, was like the highest converting thing by miles.
You know, was like retargeting – even retargeting like lower intent like traffic, you know, could be an interesting thing.
Yeah, I mean we'll do all that sort of stuff.

That stuff just takes time.
It's like people have to get the product and they have to consume it for a little bit and they start posting about it.
But that's why we'll start to spin.
But it's not going to be the fire hose, which is like dollars in, dollars out.
And so we just need that equation to be balanced.
And once we get the CPMs again to a reasonable spot, it seems very reasonable that there's like a there there.
But until then, it's like, we're just, it's tough.
It's just, it's hard to, it's hard to get the accounts to where we need them to be.
So maybe that connects a little bit like full circle, right?
Because your brand new domain, you have literally like a hundred backlinks on your domain.

And I think like we, you all, and with some of our help, I think set a good foundation for, you know, from a brand perspective, the content, the substance, like, you know, it gives you a feeling of like, this is a...
You know, business, right?
And, like, thoughtful and intentional.
And I think, like, we've had some pretty decent experience with sites that are, like, brand new.
And normally, like, we wouldn't do, like, essentially, like, it's a disqualifier for us.
Like, anyone that comes to us now would be like, we don't have an existing site or a site is, like, less than a year old.
it's a disqualifier just because, like, most people don't have the patience to go do the grunt work that it takes to set a good foundation.
And the foundation takes a while.
You can't just, like, you know, hit a button or, you know.
But it's also, like, we've seen when we were talking to him and hers, like, they were spending $100 million in ads a year.
And, like, that drug gets so intense.
It's, like, so hard to get off the drug.
Or not even get off, like, not be where the entirety of your business is dependent on, like, meta.
And then meta deciding to F with your, you know, business.
then in one day, like, could, like, one month, essentially, your business could implode if, like.
We're to mess with their algorithm somehow and impact it directly, you know?

And so it's like, that's the, the way I look at it is like, hedge, the earlier you hedge, the, it's going to take a while, but then once you hedge, then the more leverage you have in using like paid as a fuel on things that are already working, you know, like Canva, I like Canva as the best example of that, where they've been profitable now for 10 years.
They have 250 million visitors a month and that traffic is worth over $100 million a month in paid traffic alone, right?

don't think you all have stuff to think about.

think it was, like, June, July, or just?

so, but with that being said, like, I think, like, it's okay if it's, like, the business is so early and it was, like, the kind of deal that we wouldn't have even done just because it's, like, on launch, there's no website, essentially.

are we going going to

And we just see this as, like, a future, like, growth area for us in this space and also, like, to kind of validate.

would hurt almost like all...
I'm being transparent, right?
As a business owner of like...

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Discuss Biologica's ad challenges and the future of the Biologica-GrowthX partnership.](https://fathom.video/share/ajyjTMZxugJhZSSDW5rpmpxti69VfSja?tab=summary&timestamp=56.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Unsustainable Meta CPMs** are stalling Biologica's growth, making their current ad strategy unprofitable.](https://fathom.video/share/ajyjTMZxugJhZSSDW5rpmpxti69VfSja?tab=summary&timestamp=80.0)
  - [**Biologica cannot renew** the contract at its current price, citing a costly 6-month "co-development" phase where they built key workflows (e.g., a regulatory review prompt) that GrowthX then integrated.](https://fathom.video/share/ajyjTMZxugJhZSSDW5rpmpxti69VfSja?tab=summary&timestamp=829.0)
  - [**GrowthX is firm on price** (current rate is their minimum) but is open to creative structures, such as performance-based milestones tied to organic growth.](https://fathom.video/share/ajyjTMZxugJhZSSDW5rpmpxti69VfSja?tab=summary&timestamp=1557.0)
  - [**The path forward** is a new organic growth strategy to build long-term authority and reduce ad dependency.](https://fathom.video/share/ajyjTMZxugJhZSSDW5rpmpxti69VfSja?tab=summary&timestamp=1225.0)

## Topics

### Biologica's Ad Performance Crisis

  - [**Problem:** Unsustainably high CPMs on Meta are making Biologica's ad spend unprofitable.](https://fathom.video/share/ajyjTMZxugJhZSSDW5rpmpxti69VfSja?tab=summary&timestamp=80.0)
  - [**Impact:** This stalls growth and forces a pivot to other channels (e.g., Google Ads), which are slower to scale.](https://fathom.video/share/ajyjTMZxugJhZSSDW5rpmpxti69VfSja?tab=summary&timestamp=207.0)
  - [**Rationale:** Biologica is meeting with a Meta rep to diagnose the issue, believing it's either an internal fix or a platform-level anomaly.](https://fathom.video/share/ajyjTMZxugJhZSSDW5rpmpxti69VfSja?tab=summary&timestamp=156.0)

### Partnership Challenges & Renewal

  - [**Biologica's Stance:** Cannot renew the contract at its current price.](https://fathom.video/share/ajyjTMZxugJhZSSDW5rpmpxti69VfSja?tab=summary&timestamp=932.0)
  - [**Justification:** The first 6 months were a costly "co-development" phase.](https://fathom.video/share/ajyjTMZxugJhZSSDW5rpmpxti69VfSja?tab=summary&timestamp=840.0)
      - [Biologica built key workflows (e.g., a regulatory review prompt) that GrowthX then integrated.](https://fathom.video/share/ajyjTMZxugJhZSSDW5rpmpxti69VfSja?tab=summary&timestamp=1291.0)
      - [This process was expensive in both cost and team cycles.](https://fathom.video/share/ajyjTMZxugJhZSSDW5rpmpxti69VfSja?tab=summary&timestamp=850.0)
  - [**GrowthX's Stance:** Cannot lower the price.](https://fathom.video/share/ajyjTMZxugJhZSSDW5rpmpxti69VfSja?tab=summary&timestamp=1557.0)
      - [**Rationale:** The current rate is their minimum ($15k/mo). A downsell would hurt key business metrics (ACV, margins) and force them to turn down higher-paying clients.](https://fathom.video/share/ajyjTMZxugJhZSSDW5rpmpxti69VfSja?tab=summary&timestamp=1557.0)
  - [**Mutual Interest:** Both parties want to continue the partnership, recognizing Biologica's value as a "lighthouse" client in the wellness/healthcare vertical.](https://fathom.video/share/ajyjTMZxugJhZSSDW5rpmpxti69VfSja?tab=summary&timestamp=758.0)

### Proposed Solution: Organic Growth Strategy

  - [**Goal:** Build long-term organic authority to reduce ad dependency.](https://fathom.video/share/ajyjTMZxugJhZSSDW5rpmpxti69VfSja?tab=summary&timestamp=1225.0)
  - [**Method:** A foundational SEO strategy focused on:](https://fathom.video/share/ajyjTMZxugJhZSSDW5rpmpxti69VfSja?tab=summary&timestamp=455.0)
      - [**Content:** High-quality, medical-grade content to build authority.](https://fathom.video/share/ajyjTMZxugJhZSSDW5rpmpxti69VfSja?tab=summary&timestamp=653.0)
      - [**Backlinks:** A deliberate strategy to earn links, as a new domain (100 backlinks) faces an uphill battle in the health space.](https://fathom.video/share/ajyjTMZxugJhZSSDW5rpmpxti69VfSja?tab=summary&timestamp=455.0)
  - [**Incentive Structure:** GrowthX proposed performance-based milestones tied to organic growth metrics (e.g., AI visibility, site traffic) to align incentives.](https://fathom.video/share/ajyjTMZxugJhZSSDW5rpmpxti69VfSja?tab=summary&timestamp=1626.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**GrowthX:** Deliver a proposal for a new organic growth strategy, including a creative incentive structure (e.g., performance milestones) at the current minimum price.](https://fathom.video/share/ajyjTMZxugJhZSSDW5rpmpxti69VfSja?tab=summary&timestamp=1659.0)

action_items.description: Email Kirkland Maggie's regulatory prompt for post-processing; request implementation timeline
Email Brian post-contract proposal: analysis, growth plan, milestones, pricing (min flat)
